enrichment.short_description: Appcues is an in-app engagement platform for building onboarding tours, checklists, surveys, and messages to drive feature adoption.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Appcues helps product teams build in-app tours, checklists, surveys, and messages to onboard users and drive feature adoption.

features.title: In-App Tours & Flows
features.description: Guided walkthroughs for onboarding new users and driving feature adoption.

features.title: Dynamic Content Library
features.description: Reusable in-app messages, surveys, offers, and guides.

features.title: User Segmentation
features.description: Target experiences to specific groups of users.

features.title: AI-Assisted Experience Building
features.description: AI recommendations to help build and optimize in-app campaigns.

features.title: Analytics & Behavioral Insights
features.description: Track user engagement and campaign performance.

features.title: In-App Surveys
features.description: Collect user feedback directly inside the product.

pros: All plans include the same core feature set (experience building, targeting, analytics, and AI) from the Start tier up
Dedicated Spark plan tailored to small teams (under 25 people) shipping their first in-app experiences
AI-assisted campaign building and optimization recommendations
Reporting history scales up to 36 months on the Enterprise plan
cons: No published pricing on the website; every plan requires booking a call to get a quote
Pricing is MAU-based, so costs scale directly with monthly active users
Number of published experiences is capped by plan tier (10/25/100)

faqs.question: Does Appcues publish its pricing?
faqs.answer: No. Appcues pricing is based on monthly active users (MAUs) and requires booking a call; specific dollar amounts are not published on the site.

faqs.question: What plans does Appcues offer?
faqs.answer: Appcues offers Start, Grow, and Enterprise plans, plus a Spark plan for small teams, which differ by MAU volume, number of published experiences, and reporting history length.

faqs.question: Does every Appcues plan include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, according to Appcues, all plans include experience building, user targeting, analytics, and AI capabilities from day one.

faqs.question: Who is the Spark plan for?
faqs.answer: Spark is built for small teams (under 25 people) shipping their first in-app experiences.

use_cases.title: User Onboarding
use_cases.description: Build guided in-app tours and checklists to walk new users through key setup steps.

use_cases.title: Feature Adoption
use_cases.description: Use targeted in-app messages and tooltips to drive adoption of underused features.

use_cases.title: In-App Feedback Collection
use_cases.description: Deploy surveys inside the product to gather user feedback and sentiment.

features: Built for small teams (under 25 people) shipping their first in-app experiences

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Appcues?
paragraphs: Appcues is an in-app engagement platform that helps product and customer success teams build onboarding tours, checklists, surveys, and in-app messages without needing engineering involvement for every change.
The platform includes user segmentation, campaign performance analytics, and AI-assisted tools for building and optimizing in-app experiences.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Appcues is for
paragraphs: Appcues is aimed at product teams, customer success managers, and growth teams at SaaS companies that want to drive user activation and feature adoption through in-app experiences.

enrichment.short_description: Chameleon is an in-app guidance platform combining behavior tracking, product tours, and AI-built experiences to reduce friction and drive adoption.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Chameleon offers in-app product tours, tooltips, surveys, and AI-built guidance (Copilot) to onboard users and drive feature adoption.

features.title: Compass
features.description: Monitors user behavior to find where users get stuck in the product.

features.title: Copilot
features.description: AI tool that builds in-app guidance automatically and runs analysis.

features.title: Prism
features.description: Personalizes experiences for individual users (1:1 personalization).

features.title: Ranger
features.description: Flags stale flows, broken elements, and inactive seats.

features.title: In-App Guidance Types
features.description: Tours, tooltips, surveys, launchers, banners, and checklists.

features.title: A/B Testing & Segmentation
features.description: Test and target guidance to specific user segments.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with HubSpot, Salesforce, Mixpanel, Heap, Figma, Slack, Linear, Snowflake, and Amplitude.

pros: Copilot AI can auto-build in-app guidance rather than requiring fully manual creation
Ranger automatically flags stale or broken flows for upkeep
Wide integration list including Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Linear, and Snowflake
14-day free trial with no credit card required, including Enterprise-level features
cons: Entry-level Pro plan starts around $750/month for 5,000 MTUs, a meaningful commitment for very small teams
Enterprise plan pricing is custom and requires booking a demo
Additional seats beyond plan limits cost extra ($300/month per 5 seats on Pro)

faqs.question: Does Chameleon offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Chameleon offers a 14-day free trial with full Enterprise-level access and no credit card required.

faqs.question: What is Copilot?
faqs.answer: Copilot is Chameleon's AI-powered tool for automatically building in-app guidance and running analysis.

faqs.question: What does Chameleon integrate with?
faqs.answer: Chameleon integrates with tools including HubSpot, Salesforce, Mixpanel, Heap, Figma, Slack, and Linear, plus data warehouses like Snowflake.

faqs.question: How is Chameleon priced?
faqs.answer: Chameleon's published plans (Startup, Pro, Growth) are priced by monthly tracked users (MTUs), starting at $279/month for the Startup plan at 2,000 MTUs; Enterprise pricing is custom.

use_cases.title: In-App Onboarding
use_cases.description: Launch tours, tooltips, and checklists to guide new users through key product steps.

use_cases.title: Friction Detection
use_cases.description: Use Compass to monitor behavior and identify where users get stuck in the product.

use_cases.title: Automatic Guidance Creation
use_cases.description: Use Copilot to auto-generate in-app guidance instead of building each flow manually.

use_cases.title: Personalized Experiences
use_cases.description: Use Prism to tailor in-app content to individual users or segments.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Chameleon?
paragraphs: Chameleon is an in-app guidance platform that combines behavior monitoring (Compass), AI-built guidance (Copilot), 1:1 personalization (Prism), and flow maintenance alerts (Ranger) into one system for improving product experiences.
It supports guidance formats such as tours, tooltips, surveys, launchers, banners, and checklists, along with A/B testing and user segmentation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Chameleon is for
paragraphs: Chameleon is built for product managers, product marketers, and product designers at SaaS companies who want to reduce user friction, drive feature adoption, and personalize in-app experiences without heavy engineering involvement.

name: Userflow
skip_reason: The official site (userflow.com and www.userflow.com) returned HTTP 403 Forbidden to automated fetches on the homepage, pricing, and FAQ pages, so content could not be verified directly from the primary source.

enrichment.short_description: Product Fruits is a no-code platform for building product tours, checklists, tooltips, and AI-powered onboarding flows to drive feature adoption.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Product Fruits offers no-code product tours, checklists, in-app announcements, and an AI onboarding copilot to boost user adoption.

features.title: Flows
features.description: Multichannel, AI-powered onboarding that adjusts to each user in real time.

features.title: Elvin Copilot
features.description: AI assistant that automatically resolves user support questions.

features.title: Product Tours & Guided Experiences
features.description: Step-by-step in-app walkthroughs.

features.title: Onboarding Checklists
features.description: Track user setup progress.

features.title: Hints & Tooltips
features.description: Contextual in-app help.

features.title: In-App Announcements
features.description: Communicate product updates directly in the product.

features.title: NPS Surveys & Feedback
features.description: Collect user sentiment inside the app.

features.title: Knowledge Base
features.description: Searchable self-service help articles.

pros: Elvin Copilot is claimed by the vendor to automatically resolve 93% of support questions
25% discount for annual billing across paid plans
Free trial available on every plan
Rated 4.7/5 on G2 and 4.8/5 on Capterra per the vendor's site
cons: Pricing scales with monthly active users (MAUs), so costs rise as your user base grows
Automatic translations and custom events are gated to the Pro tier and above
Enterprise-grade options (SAML SSO, security audit, SLA) require a custom plan

faqs.question: Does Product Fruits require coding to use?
faqs.answer: No, Product Fruits is built as a no-code platform for creating onboarding flows, tours, and other in-app content.

faqs.question: What is Elvin Copilot?
faqs.answer: Elvin Copilot is Product Fruits' AI assistant that automatically answers user support questions inside the product.

faqs.question: Does Product Fruits offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, all plans include free trial access.

faqs.question: How much does Product Fruits cost?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan starts at $96/month ($72/month billed annually), with Pro and Business plans at $149/month ($112/month annually); custom plans are available for larger organizations.

use_cases.title: Adaptive Onboarding
use_cases.description: Use Flows to deliver AI-powered onboarding that adjusts to each user's behavior in real time.

use_cases.title: Support Deflection
use_cases.description: Use Elvin Copilot to automatically resolve common in-app support questions.

use_cases.title: Feature Adoption
use_cases.description: Use hints, tooltips, and announcements to surface underused features to existing users.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Product Fruits?
paragraphs: Product Fruits is a no-code platform that lets teams build product tours, checklists, tooltips, and in-app announcements to onboard users and drive feature adoption.
Its Flows feature delivers AI-powered onboarding that adapts to each user, and its Elvin Copilot AI assistant automatically resolves common in-app support questions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Product Fruits is for
paragraphs: Product Fruits targets Customer Success teams, Product Managers, and growth/product marketing teams at SaaS, enterprise software, and education platforms who need onboarding without engineering resources.

enrichment.short_description: UserGuiding is a no-code platform for building product tours, checklists, tooltips, and an AI support assistant to drive user onboarding and adoption.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: UserGuiding offers no-code product tours, onboarding checklists, tooltips, and an AI assistant to help SaaS teams onboard and retain users.

features.title: Product Tours
features.description: Interactive guided experiences showing product value.

features.title: Onboarding Checklists
features.description: Step-by-step completion lists to improve conversion.

features.title: Hotspots & Tooltips
features.description: Non-intrusive contextual UI annotations.

features.title: Banners
features.description: Persistent announcement bars for updates and promotions.

features.title: In-App Surveys
features.description: Quick in-product feedback collection.

features.title: Knowledge Base & Resource Center
features.description: Self-service help documentation.

features.title: AI Assistant
features.description: 24/7 multilingual in-app support agent.

features.title: Session Replay
features.description: Records user interactions for review.

pros: Free forever 'Support Essentials' plan includes knowledge base, resource center, and 50 free AI Assistant resolutions
30-day money-back guarantee on paid plans
14-day free trial before converting to paid or free tier
AI Assistant supports multilingual in-app support
cons: A/B testing, custom CSS, and localization are gated to the Growth plan and above
Enterprise compliance features (SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA, SAML SSO) require custom Enterprise pricing
Pricing scales with Monthly Active Users (MAU), so cost increases as your user base grows

faqs.question: Does UserGuiding have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Support Essentials' plan is free forever and includes the Knowledge Base, Product Updates, Resource Center, and 50 free AI Assistant resolutions.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, UserGuiding offers a 14-day free trial before plans convert to paid or the free tier.

faqs.question: What languages does the UserGuiding site support?
faqs.answer: UserGuiding's site is available in English, Portuguese (BR), Spanish, and French.

faqs.question: How is UserGuiding priced?
faqs.answer: Pricing is based on Monthly Active Users (MAU), with the Starter plan starting at $174/month billed yearly.

use_cases.title: Product Onboarding
use_cases.description: Use product tours and checklists to guide new users toward activation.

use_cases.title: Self-Service Support
use_cases.description: Deploy a knowledge base and AI Assistant to reduce support tickets.

use_cases.title: Feature Announcements
use_cases.description: Use banners and hotspots to announce new features to existing users.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is UserGuiding?
paragraphs: UserGuiding is a no-code product adoption platform that lets teams build product tours, onboarding checklists, hotspots, tooltips, and banners without writing code.
It also includes a knowledge base, resource center, and a 24/7 multilingual AI Assistant for self-service support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who UserGuiding is for
paragraphs: UserGuiding targets product teams and SaaS companies that want to improve onboarding and reduce support costs, and it offers a free forever plan for teams starting with basic self-service support.

name: Usetiful
skip_reason: Usetiful has been acquired by Fullstory and is no longer offered as a standalone product for new customers. The site shows an acquisition banner, its pricing page redirects to Fullstory's acquisition announcement, and new visitors are pointed to 'Fullstory Guides and Surveys' instead of a Usetiful signup; only existing customers retain login access.

enrichment.short_description: WalkMe is an enterprise digital adoption platform providing in-app guidance, workflow automation, and analytics across enterprise applications.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: WalkMe offers AI-powered in-app guidance, workflow automation, and analytics to help enterprises adopt Salesforce, SAP, Workday, and more.

features.title: DeepUI Technology
features.description: Understands on-screen context to deliver guidance.

features.title: WalkMe AI
features.description: Omnipresent contextual assistance across applications.

features.title: Omnichannel Support
features.description: Guidance delivered across desktop, mobile, and web.

features.title: Analytics Suite
features.description: Application usage, workflow, and form analytics plus license optimization.

features.title: In-App Guidance
features.description: Conversational interfaces and step-by-step walkthroughs.

features.title: Workflow Automation
features.description: Automates and accelerates repetitive tasks.

features.title: Cross-Application Workflows
features.description: Execute workflows that span multiple connected applications.

pros: Supports major enterprise apps out of the box: Salesforce, Workday, SAP, Microsoft Dynamics 365, ServiceNow, and SuccessFactors
Available across desktop, mobile, and web
DeepUI technology reads on-screen context to power guidance
cons: No published pricing; all plans require requesting a quote
Starting January 2027, certain AI capabilities will require additional 'AI Units', with no pricing yet disclosed
Positioned for enterprise buyers, which may not suit small teams

faqs.question: Does WalkMe publish its pricing?
faqs.answer: No, WalkMe uses quote-based pricing; the site directs visitors to 'Request a quote' rather than listing plan prices.

faqs.question: What applications does WalkMe support out of the box?
faqs.answer: WalkMe lists support for Salesforce, Workday, SAP, Microsoft Dynamics 365, ServiceNow, and SuccessFactors.

faqs.question: What languages does WalkMe support?
faqs.answer: WalkMe's site lists English, French, German, and Japanese.

faqs.question: Will WalkMe's AI features change in pricing?
faqs.answer: WalkMe states that starting January 2027, certain AI capabilities will require 'AI Units,' though pricing for these has not been disclosed.

use_cases.title: Employee Software Adoption
use_cases.description: Guide employees through enterprise systems like SAP, Workday, and Salesforce with in-app walkthroughs.

use_cases.title: Customer-Facing Guidance
use_cases.description: Add contextual help and automation to customer-facing web and mobile applications.

use_cases.title: Workflow Automation
use_cases.description: Automate repetitive multi-step tasks across connected enterprise applications.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is WalkMe?
paragraphs: WalkMe is a digital adoption platform that layers in-app guidance, workflow automation, and analytics onto enterprise applications such as Salesforce, SAP, Workday, and ServiceNow.
Its DeepUI technology reads on-screen context to power contextual assistance, and WalkMe AI provides omnipresent support across desktop, mobile, and web.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who WalkMe is for
paragraphs: WalkMe is built for IT, HR, sales, and customer care teams at enterprises undergoing digital transformation and needing to drive adoption of complex internal or customer-facing software.

enrichment.short_description: Whatfix is a digital adoption platform offering in-app guidance, simulated training via Mirror, and product analytics for enterprise software.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Whatfix provides in-app guidance, simulated training (Mirror), and product analytics to help enterprises drive software adoption.

features.title: In-App Guidance
features.description: Flows, Smart Tips, Pop-Ups, and Task Lists for contextual walkthroughs and prompts.

features.title: Content Lifecycle Management
features.description: Governance controls for managing in-app content over time.

features.title: Mirror
features.description: Clones your application for training, AI roleplay scenarios, and guided simulations.

features.title: Adaptive Assessments
features.description: Skills assessments delivered through Mirror.

features.title: Product Analytics
features.description: AutoCapture event collection, session replay, and real-time cohorts.

features.title: Guidance Analytics
features.description: Measures the effectiveness of in-app content.

pros: Covers both in-app guidance (DAP) and a separate Product Analytics offering with AutoCapture, session replay, and cohorts
Mirror lets teams clone the application for training and simulation instead of practicing in production
Over 700 customers across 30+ countries with a claimed 99.5% CSAT per the vendor
G2 rating of 4.6 stars across 545 reviews per the vendor's site
cons: No published pricing; every plan requires a demo or sales conversation
The free Product Analytics Standard plan is only available bundled with a paid DAP plan
Positioned primarily for large enterprises across regulated industries

faqs.question: Does Whatfix have published pricing?
faqs.answer: No. Whatfix pricing combines a flat fee plus user license fees and requires requesting a demo; specific amounts aren't published.

faqs.question: What is Mirror?
faqs.answer: Mirror is Whatfix's application-cloning feature used for training simulations, AI roleplay scenarios, and adaptive assessments.

faqs.question: Does Whatfix offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: A free 'Standard' Product Analytics plan exists, but only as an add-on bundled with a paid DAP Web & Desktop plan.

faqs.question: What industries does Whatfix serve?
faqs.answer: Whatfix lists customers across banking, education, financial services, healthcare, insurance, pharma & life sciences, and the public sector.

use_cases.title: Employee Onboarding & Training
use_cases.description: Use Mirror to clone applications and run guided simulations or AI roleplay for new hires.

use_cases.title: In-App Guidance for Software Rollouts
use_cases.description: Deploy Flows, Smart Tips, and Task Lists to guide users through new or complex software.

use_cases.title: Usage Analytics
use_cases.description: Use AutoCapture and session replay to understand how employees or customers use an application.

features: DAP Web & Desktop, or Product Analytics (free only when bundled with a paid DAP plan)
DAP Web & Desktop, Product Analytics, or Mirror for Web Apps
Multi-app plans for employee or customer-facing applications

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Whatfix?
paragraphs: Whatfix is a digital adoption platform combining in-app guidance (Flows, Smart Tips, Pop-Ups, Task Lists) with Mirror, a feature that clones applications for training simulations and AI roleplay, and a separate Product Analytics suite.
Whatfix serves enterprise customers across banking, healthcare, insurance, education, and the public sector.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Whatfix is for
paragraphs: Whatfix targets L&D, HR, sales, and product teams at large enterprises that need in-app guidance, training simulations, and usage analytics for complex internal or customer-facing software.

enrichment.short_description: Apty is an AI-powered digital adoption platform adding in-app guidance, walkthroughs, and workflow analytics to apps like Salesforce and Workday.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Apty is a digital adoption platform offering AI-assisted in-app guidance, tooltips, and analytics for enterprise apps like Salesforce and ServiceNow.

features.title: No-Code Content Creation & Walkthroughs
features.description: Build in-app guidance without writing code.

features.title: Validations & Tooltips
features.description: Contextual, field-level guidance inside applications.

features.title: Adoption Analytics
features.description: Usage and process analytics with completion and friction tracking.

features.title: Live Workflow Monitoring
features.description: Real-time engagement tracking with early risk and compliance alerts.

features.title: Role-Based Access & Governance
features.description: Versioning and approval workflows for content changes.

features.title: Enterprise Integrations & API
features.description: Connects with 100+ enterprise applications.

pros: Supports 1,000+ applications with 100+ pre-built integrations, including Salesforce, Workday, ServiceNow, Microsoft Dynamics, Coupa, and Infor
Includes multi-language and user segmentation support
Includes early risk and compliance alerting for workflow monitoring
cons: Starting price of $9,500 per app annually may be cost-prohibitive for small teams
Final pricing depends on custom factors (employee count, implementation complexity, compliance needs), so total cost isn't fully transparent upfront
White-label and on-premise authoring are separate paid add-ons

faqs.question: How much does Apty cost?
faqs.answer: Apty's pricing starts at $9,500 per app annually, with the final price depending on user count, implementation complexity, and compliance requirements.

faqs.question: What applications does Apty integrate with?
faqs.answer: Apty integrates with 100+ enterprise applications, including Salesforce, Workday, ServiceNow, Microsoft Dynamics, Coupa, and Infor, and supports 1,000+ applications overall.

faqs.question: Is Apty no-code?
faqs.answer: Yes, Apty's content creation and walkthroughs are built with no-code tools.

faqs.question: Does Apty offer white-label or on-premise options?
faqs.answer: Yes, On-Premise Authoring and White-Label are available as optional paid add-ons.

use_cases.title: Enterprise Software Adoption
use_cases.description: Guide employees through complex systems like Salesforce, Workday, and ServiceNow with in-app walkthroughs.

use_cases.title: Compliance Monitoring
use_cases.description: Use live workflow monitoring to catch early risk and compliance issues in business-critical processes.

use_cases.title: Process Analytics
use_cases.description: Track completion and friction across critical workflows to identify where employees get stuck.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Apty?
paragraphs: Apty is an AI-powered digital adoption platform that adds no-code in-app guidance, validations, and tooltips to enterprise applications, along with live workflow monitoring and adoption analytics.
It supports 1,000+ applications with over 100 pre-built integrations, including Salesforce, Workday, ServiceNow, Microsoft Dynamics, Coupa, and Infor.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Apty is for
paragraphs: Apty is built for IT leaders, training leaders, department managers, and digital transformation teams at enterprises that need to drive adoption and compliance across multiple business applications.

name: June
skip_reason: June discontinued as a standalone product on August 8, 2025 when it joined Amplitude; the site confirms existing customers are being migrated to Amplitude and the product is no longer offered under the June name.
